---
title: "Session 04 teaching notes: Python 1"
session_id: session-04
event_id: null
---

### Session 4 — Python and pandas basics


## Introductory discussion

- Prior programming experience?
- Why go beyond Excel and use Python? (advanced analytics: Regression, prediction, ML, AI, ...)
- Why shouldn't we just use ChatGPT? (prompt adequately, validate and improve, Python: effective prompting possible)

- Advantage of Python: libraries! (PyPI)
- Interpreted: vs. compiled language


TODO: add spyder setup

TBD: quarto-include code fragments? add explanations (asides?)

Python:
- motivation: existing libraries!
- Don't repeat yourself principle of programming: by using Python libraries (modularization of code), we benefit from the code written by others.
  Illustration (whiteboard): the Python code that is involved in a typical ML studyis roughly 2 million LOC. 99.99% is imported (Pandas, scikitlearn, ...) and a few hundred LOC are our code (tip of the iceberg, we orchestrate existing libraries). For the sake of the argument, we simply ignore all of the other code, the Python core or the code that runs the OS and operates the hardware.
  It is not just LOC, but the 99% of the code (the libraries) are often very well designed and maintained (if you pick the right libraries). Also, they are constantly updated so we get performance improvements, remaining bugs are fixed, and the documentation gets better.
  This means we can really "stand on the shoulders of giants". Pretty amazing.

TODO: use Anaconda (be prepared to answer questions)
spytertest.py (in canvas) mit studis am Anfang der python session step-by-step durchgehen
use setwd() for spyder


See `1_Pandas.py` - contains code for all slides (TODO: split)

p.13: abhängig von Zeit: in-session or homework

TODO: check pandas version? 3.0?

Aufgaben: komplett (soweit die Zeit da ist)
Bei Zeitnot: Aufgaben nach Haus geben
Python Lösungen: werden von Tutoren besprochen


TODO: see also `Versicherungsvertreter.py` and `Versicherungsvertreter.xslx`

## Exercises

| Page(s) | Exercise               | Summary                                                                                                          | Files                                                                                      | In-class / take-home |
| ------: | ---------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------- |
|       5 | Basic operators        | Run arithmetic, division, modulo, exponentiation, and string-concatenation expressions.                          | —                                                                                          |                      |
|      11 | Trace program output   | Predict the results of assignments, reassignment, multiple assignment, and arithmetic expressions.               | —                                                                                          |                      |
|      13 | Basic formula programs | Calculate a circle’s area and the future value of an investment using variables defined in the program.          | `CircleArea1.py`<br>`FutureValue.py`                                                       |                      |
|      22 | Import and export data | Load country and insurance data, inspect DataFrame dimensions and columns, and export the insurance data to CSV. | `countries.csv`<br>`countries.py`<br>`insurance.xlsx`<br>`insurance.py`<br>`insurance.csv` |                      |


::: {.teaching-break}
☕ Break — 10 minutes
:::

## Summary and announcements (next session)

TODO

::: {.callout-tip title="Notes for improvement"}
Take notes (improvements, common questions) on a separate paper and add to `feedback.qmd` after the session.
:::
